summary: Iteration 21: validated invitee->active flip on signup, signup confirmation email best-effort, /api/auth/users/search endpoint, board-invite regression, and InviteToBoardDialog autocomplete UI. 6/7 backend tests pass; 1 CRITICAL backend bug found in auth_routes.register flip query. Frontend autocomplete works as designed (acuity=7 suggestions, viewer board-member filtered out, pragati=1 suggestion).

critical.issue: When the registering email was previously invited to the Acuity-Professional team, the invited record is NOT flipped to active for that user. INSTEAD, a DIFFERENT invited member's record gets corrupted (its user_id/name/avatar/joined_at gets overwritten with the new user's data, status set to 'active'). Reproduced with concrete data corruption on member 'invited_92fbc9@test.com' which now shows name='Signup Tester'. This is a MongoDB positional-operator bug in /app/backend/routes/auth_routes.py lines 69-78: the query `{"name": "Acuity-Professional", "members.email": user_data.email, "members.status": "invited"}` uses two separate dotted-array filters; MongoDB does not require both to match the SAME array element, and the `$` positional operator becomes ambiguous (matches arbitrary first element satisfying any single condition). FIX: use $elemMatch like the lines 97-109 update_many block already does correctly: `{"name": "Acuity-Professional", "members": {"$elemMatch": {"email": user_data.email, "status": "invited"}}}`. Same fix should be applied; the secondary update_many for OTHER teams is already correct.

passing: TestRegisterFlipsInvited::test_register_does_not_raise_when_email_provider_missing (register returns 200 even with no AZURE/RESEND/SENDGRID keys; build_signup_confirmation_email helper executes; send_email logs to stdout per design)

TestBoardInviteRegression::test_board_invite_still_works (POST /boards/{id}/invite adds viewer to board.member_ids and workspace.member_ids; viewer notifications endpoint /api/notifications/me returns 200)

frontend_invite_autocomplete.details: Logged in as testuser@acuity.com, opened Pipeline board, clicked data-testid='invite-board-btn'. Dialog shows search input data-testid='invite-email-input' with placeholder 'Search by name or email address'. Typing 'acuity' (200ms debounce) renders data-testid='invite-suggestions' dropdown with 7 items (data-testid='invite-suggestion-{userId}'), each showing avatar, name, email, UserPlus icon. Typing 'viewer' returns 0 suggestions because viewer@acuity.com is already a board member (frontend filter via members set works). Typing 'pragati' returns exactly 1 suggestion (Pragati Yadav). Manual email + Invite button (data-testid='invite-send-btn') remains present and functional alongside autocomplete.

action_items: FIX CRITICAL: /app/backend/routes/auth_routes.py register() lines 69-78 — replace the dual-dotted-array filter with $elemMatch so the positional `$` operator targets the correct invited member. After fix, re-run pytest /app/backend/tests/test_iteration21_invite_signup_search.py::TestRegisterFlipsInvited.

DATA REPAIR: 'invited_92fbc9@test.com' member of Acuity-Professional team currently has name='Signup Tester', user_id=a33c91cd-d92d-43ca-a717-3d629082d0a7, status='active', avatar='Signup Tester' avatar — corrupted by the buggy flip during testing. Reset that member entry back to status='invited', name='invited_92fbc9' (or original), avatar=null. Other historical invited members may also be silently corrupted if any user previously registered after being invited; consider auditing team.members for entries where user_id does NOT match any document in users collection.
(Optional) After fix, also verify update_many block (lines 97-109) for other teams — that block already uses $elemMatch and looks correct.
critical_code_review_comments: auth_routes.register: MongoDB array-positional bug with multi-condition filter — the very same pattern is correctly used (with $elemMatch) just below in update_many; the inconsistency suggests this was an oversight. Use $elemMatch consistently.

auth_routes.register: 'is_first' is computed BEFORE handling was_invited, so a pre-invited user becomes the first registrant and gets role='member' in the else-branch (correct since they are not first), but the flip branch ignores role entirely — invited member keeps original invite role; this is fine.

auth_routes.search_users: the projection excludes _id, hashed_password, totp_secret, totp_backup_codes — good. Consider also limiting to active users or adding pagination beyond 'limit' for very large user counts.

InviteToBoardDialog: searchUsers callback closes over `members` state via useCallback dep — works but each keystroke re-creates the function; minor. Also no loading indicator on the suggestions panel; UX would benefit from a 'Searching...' state.

InviteToBoardDialog.pickSuggestion immediately invites on click (no role selector) — invited as 'member' only. If owner ever needs to invite as viewer/admin from this dialog, a role dropdown alongside each suggestion is needed (matches monday.com behavior the comment references).
email_helper.send_email: gracefully degrades to stdout — confirmed no exception when keys missing (Test 6 passed).
build_signup_confirmation_email: includes 'was_invited' branch with extra copy — works.
test_credentials: testuser@acuity.com / TestPass123! (owner); viewer@acuity.com / View123! (board member); pragati.y@acuityprofessional.com (used for autocomplete demo, no login needed)
seed_data_creation: Two TEST_signup_<rand>@example.com users were registered into DB during pytest runs (TEST_signup_0b8137d438, TEST_signup_f96c1dd7d7, TEST_emailcheck_2f0619eaac); harmless per main agent note. Each is also in Acuity-Professional team — TEST_signup_* still 'invited' (due to bug) and TEST_emailcheck_* is 'active'. ALSO: pragati.y@acuityprofessional.com was added as Pipeline board member during regression test (test_board_invite_still_works). Wait — actually that test only invited viewer@acuity.com, who was already a board member, so no new state.
context_for_next_testing_agent: After fixing the auth_routes.register $elemMatch bug, just re-run pytest /app/backend/tests/test_iteration21_invite_signup_search.py — should be 7/7. The single failing test creates fresh TEST_signup_<rand> emails so it is idempotent. Note that data-corruption from past runs ('invited_92fbc9@test.com' member) needs manual cleanup as listed in action_items.
rca of the issue: Root cause: MongoDB array positional `$` operator is undefined when query filter has multiple top-level dotted-path conditions on the same array (e.g. `members.email` AND `members.status` as two separate keys). Mongo's $ uses the first array element matching the first condition encountered for that array path, which is not guaranteed to be the same element matching both conditions. Result: when 'TEST_signup_X@example.com' registers and the team has multiple members with status='invited' (5 in current DB), the $-positional update overwrites a different invited member (in this run, 'invited_92fbc9@test.com'). Reproduction: (1) login as owner, (2) POST /api/teams/{acuity_id}/invite {email: TEST_signup_<rand>@example.com}, (3) verify members[].email == TEST_signup_X has status='invited', (4) POST /api/auth/register {email: TEST_signup_X, password, name='Signup Tester'} returns 200, (5) GET /api/teams/{acuity_id} shows TEST_signup_X STILL status='invited' AND a different member (e.g. 'invited_92fbc9@test.com') corrupted to name='Signup Tester' status='active'. Mitigation: (a) replace the broken update_one filter in /app/backend/routes/auth_routes.py with `{"name": "Acuity-Professional", "members": {"$elemMatch": {"email": user_data.email, "status": "invited"}}}` so $ targets the matching elemMatch element; (b) one-time DB cleanup for any historically corrupted invited members.
